jobs.basic_qualifications:
- 1+ years of software development experience
- Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, Software Engineering, MIS, or a related field
- 1+ years of professional experience building scalable and extensible production software systems with Java, object-oriented design and design patterns
- 1+ years of enterprise level web content development & delivery with full SDLC; including experience implementing web Content Management Systems (CMS) serving very large number of user base
- 1+ years of experience implementing Adobe AEM 6.5 and AEP
jobs.description:
We’re on the lookout for the curious, those who think big and want to define the world of tomorrow. At Amazon, you will grow into the high impact, visionary person you know you’re ready to be. Every day will be filled with exciting new challenges, developing new skills, and achieving personal growth. How often can you say that your work changes the world? At Amazon, you’ll say it often. Join us and define tomorrow.
Do you want to solve business challenges through innovative technology? Do you enjoy working on cutting-edge, scalable services technology in a team environment? Do you like working on industry-defining projects that move the needle?
At Amazon, we hire the best minds in technology to innovate and build on behalf of our customers. The focus we have on our customers is why we are one of the world’s most beloved brands – customer obsession is part of our company DNA. Our Application Development Engineers use third-party technologies such as Adobe AEM, Adobe Managed Services (AMS), and Adobe Experience Platform (AEP), as well as custom-built technologies to solve complex problems and get to see the impact of their work firsthand.

Key job responsibilities
• Design and implement efficient content models, security models, workflows and templates.
* Design and implement customized Adobe AEM solutions and components leveraging current Adobe AEM/Adobe Managed Services (AMS) & Adobe Experience Platform (AEP) software releases
* Deliver solutions by writing clean, efficient, and reusable code by following established coding standards.
* Customize, integrate and deliver solutions by using multiple Adobe services such as AEM, AEP and Adobe Target
* Work in an agile environment to deliver high-quality software.

A day in the life
The challenges Application Development Engineers solve for at Amazon are big and influence millions of customers, sellers, and products around the world. We are looking for individuals who are passionate about creating new products, features, and services by using Adobe AEM, Adobe Managed Services (AMS), and Adobe Experience Platform (AEP) Developer experiences, while managing ambiguity and the pace of a company where development cycles are measured in weeks, not years.
jobs.description_short: We’re on the lookout for the curious, those who think big and want to define the world of tomorrow. At Amazon, you will grow into the high impact, visionary person you know you’re ready to be. Every day

jobs.title: IT Application Development Engineer, AWS Marketing

jobs.basic_qualifications:
- 7+ years of non-internship professional software development experience
- 7+ years of programming with at least one software programming language experience
- 5+ years of leading design or architecture (design patterns, reliability and scaling) of new and existing systems experience
- Experience as a mentor, tech lead or leading an engineering team
jobs.description:
AWS Infrastructure Services owns the design, planning, delivery, and operation of all AWS global infrastructure. In other words, we’re the people who keep the cloud running. We support all AWS data centers and all of the servers, storage, networking, power, and cooling equipment that ensure our customers have continual access to the innovation they rely on. We work on the most challenging problems, with thousands of variables impacting the supply chain — and we’re looking for talented people who want to help.

You’ll join a diverse team of software, hardware, and network engineers, supply chain specialists, security experts, operations managers, and other vital roles. You’ll collaborate with people across AWS to help us deliver the highest standards for safety and security while providing seemingly infinite capacity at the lowest possible cost for our customers. And you’ll experience an inclusive culture that welcomes bold ideas and empowers you to own them to completion.

About DC Bridge:
Join the DC Bridge team in pioneering next-generation AI/ML solutions that power AWS's global data center operations. We're building cutting-edge systems that orchestrate physical work processes across AWS's worldwide data centers, directly impacting millions of customers who rely on AWS services.

Position Impact:
You'll be at the forefront of transforming data center operations through AI/ML innovations, developing intelligent systems that optimize technician workflows, automate decision-making processes, and enhance operational efficiency across AWS's global infrastructure.

Required Qualifications:
• 7+ years of software development experience with proven expertise in Python, Java, or equivalent languages
• Strong background in one or more of:
- Frontend development and UI/UX design
- Platform engineering (SDKs/Frameworks)
- ETL and large-scale data processing
- DC telemetry systems
- Machine learning (specializing in anomaly detection, classification, time series analysis)
- Solution architecture and technical advisory
• Hands-on experience with modern ML frameworks (TensorFlow, PyTorch, SageMaker, Bedrock)
• Track record of mentoring and technical leadership
• Excellence in problem-solving and communication

Ideal Candidate Profile:
• Thrives in ambiguous environments and adapts quickly to change
• Demonstrates a scrappy mindset with ability to deliver results in fast-paced settings
• Maintains deep technical expertise while staying customer-focused
• Shows passionate engagement with AI/ML advancements
• Possesses strong understanding of AI/ML technology application (LLMs, agents, RAG, Skills, ML models)
• Works autonomously and demonstrates deep problem-solving capabilities
• Balances subtle improvements with disruptive innovation when needed

This role offers the opportunity to shape the future of AWS data center operations through innovative AI/ML solutions while working with cutting-edge technologies at unprecedented scale.

Key job responsibilities
• Lead and architect the development of state-of-the-art AI/ML platforms and solutions, serving as a technical leader for both data center operations and engineering teams
• Own end-to-end delivery of technically challenging projects, including scalable ML frameworks, deployment pipelines, and intuitive interfaces for non-ML experts
• Drive operational excellence by building robust data processing pipelines and ETL systems for DC telemetry data, while identifying and addressing operational challenges early
• Mentor and grow junior engineers, acting as a force multiplier by sharing AI/ML expertise and best practices
• Lead cross-functional collaboration efforts to integrate ML solutions into existing DC workflows, while maintaining highest standards for system extensibility and scalability
• Contribute to and champion improvements in development processes, particularly in the context of ML development and deployment
• Provide technical consultation and architectural guidance to internal customers while insisting on the highest standards for long-term system sustainability
• Design and implement reusable components and tools that enhance team productivity and system reliability


About the team
About AWS

Diverse Experiences
AWS values diverse experiences. Even if you do not meet all of the preferred qualifications and skills listed in the job description, we encourage candidates to apply. If your career is just starting, hasn’t followed a traditional path, or includes alternative experiences, don’t let it stop you from applying.

Why AWS?
Amazon Web Services (AWS) is the world’s most comprehensive and broadly adopted cloud platform. We pioneered cloud computing and never stopped innovating — that’s why customers from the most successful startups to Global 500 companies trust our robust suite of products and services to power their businesses.

Inclusive Team Culture
Here at AWS, it’s in our nature to learn and be curious. Our employee-led affinity groups foster a culture of inclusion that empower us to be proud of our differences. Ongoing events and learning experiences, including our Conversations on Race and Ethnicity (CORE) and AmazeCon (gender diversity) conferences, inspire us to never stop embracing our uniqueness.

Mentorship & Career Growth
We’re continuously raising our performance bar as we strive to become Earth’s Best Employer. That’s why you’ll find endless knowledge-sharing, mentorship and other career-advancing resources here to help you develop into a better-rounded professional.

Work/Life Balance
We value work-life harmony. Achieving success at work should never come at the expense of sacrifices at home, which is why we strive for flexibility as part of our working culture. When we feel supported in the workplace and at home, there’s nothing we can’t achieve in the cloud.
jobs.description_short: AWS Infrastructure Services owns the design, planning, delivery, and operation of all AWS global infrastructure. In other words, we’re the people who keep the cloud running. We support all AWS data centers

jobs.basic_qualifications:
- 10+ years of specific technology domain areas (e.g. software development, cloud computing, systems engineering, infrastructure, security, networking, data & analytics) experience
- Bachelor's degree in computer science, engineering, mathematics or equivalent
- Experience developing technology solutions and evangelising end-to-end technology roadmaps that guide IT transformations toward cloud computing
- Experience communicating across technical and non-technical audiences and at C-level, including training, workshops, publications
- Experience with AI/ML technologies
jobs.description:
Developers and development teams are key influencers who steer organizational adoption of new technologies, services, and platforms. The mission of the Developer Acceleration (DevAx) team within PACE is to help developers gain greater familiarity and confidence building AI/Agentic applications, and to accelerate the path from prototype to production at scale.

As a Principal DevAx Solutions Architect within PACE, you will be at the forefront of emerging technology adoption, with a particular focus on Generative AI, Agentic AI, and AI-native application architectures. You will help customers understand and harness the transformative potential of services like Amazon Bedrock, Amazon Nova, and Frontier Agents, translating these forefront capabilities into practical, production-ready solutions that address real business problems. You will continuously sharpen and apply your expertise in the full AI development lifecycle to upskill developers and development teams on AWS services.

As a trusted advisor with proven technical expertise developing full-stack solutions on AWS, you will share recommendations around system and application architectures, security and cost considerations, performance, reliability, and operational efficiency to accelerate customers' most challenging, mission-critical projects.

Internally, you will be the voice of the customer. You will synthesize observations from customer engagements and market trends around custom-developed and AI-native workloads to inform the roadmap of AWS service teams.

If you are keen to help AWS customers deliver the art of the possible, come build the future with us.

Key job responsibilities
- Own the end-to-end path to production for AWS built prototypes, partnering with Prototyping Architects, customer engineering teams, and field stakeholders to ensure prototypes successfully transition from proof-of-concept to production-grade, scalable solutions on AWS driving measurable business outcomes for customers.
- Lead large-scale, cross-organizational developer enablement initiatives that drive measurable adoption of Agentic AI, AI/ML, and modern application development on AWS.
- Design and build industry-significant reference architectures and developer-focused solutions that become recognized patterns across the community.
- Decompose complex developer transformation challenges into scalable, reusable approaches.
- Influence C-level and senior engineering leadership on how GenAI can drive organizational change.
- Mentor and develop senior Prototyping Architects and DevAx practitioners.
- Lead cross-functional hiring, contribute to thought leadership through publications, conference talks, and community engagement.
- Surface strategic product feedback to engineering and PM teams.

About the team
The Prototyping and AI Customer Engineering (PACE) team is an elite group of hands-on builders within AWS. Through rapid, time-boxed prototyping engagements, PACE helps customers explore the art of the possible with AWS, turning bold ideas into working solutions that demonstrate real business value. PACE operates at the frontier of emerging technology, with deep expertise in Generative AI, Agentic AI, AI/MAL, and modern agentic application development.

jobs.basic_qualifications:
- Experience troubleshooting integrated and interdependent computer systems
- Can lift up to 49 pounds, stand/walk during shifts lasting up to 12 hours, and frequently push, pull, squat, bend, and reach
- Experience in a dynamic environment with a high degree of customer service
- 1+ years of Windows, Mac or Linux Operating systems support experience or equivalent experience
jobs.description:
Operations is at the heart of Amazon’s business. We are known for our speed, accuracy, and exceptional service. Our buildings deliver tens of thousands of products to hundreds of countries worldwide, every day. OpsTech Solutions provides nonstop global IT support to Amazon Operations as a vital piece to the puzzle. We are looking for self-motivated individuals to take on new challenges and motivate teams in fostering solutions to the hurdles we face. Together, we take ownership for what we do — whether supporting IT infrastructure, developing new technology in-house, launching a new Amazon Operations facility, or delighting our customers by delivering packages directly to them.

About The Role
As an IT Support Associate II, you will use your skills to support the operations team in resolving technical problems within an Amazon Operations facility that span across a multitude of technical disciplines. We are committed to improvement; you will be encouraged to participate in career development opportunities as presented to progress your career at Amazon.

What Do We Offer?
Amazon offers competitive compensation packages including comprehensive healthcare benefits starting on Day 1, matching 401(k) program, and up to 20 weeks of paid parental leave. But wait, there’s more: we don’t wear suits and ties! Come as you are because jeans, t-shirts, sneakers and sometimes a neon safety vest will be your daily outfit.

Key job responsibilities
Responsibilities include, but are not limited to:
- Allocating, maintaining, and troubleshooting of IT equipment used in Amazon Operations facilities.
- Handling support requests and procedures across the network.
- Assisting in training new hires.
- Continuous learning of new technologies.
- Supporting customers from multiple buildings in the area, as well as remote customers.
- Moving up to 49lbs as well as standing and walking for shifts lasting up to 12 hours.

A day in the life
Support Technicians dedicate approximately 80% of their time to troubleshooting and repairing end-user hardware within our fulfillment centers, ensuring continuous station uptime and operational efficiency. The remaining 20% involves executing projects and managed change controls to maintain or improve our infrastructure.

jobs.title: IT Support Associate II, Ops Tech Solutions (OTS)

jobs.basic_qualifications:
- 5+ years of technical product or program management experience
- 7+ years of working directly with engineering teams experience
- 5+ years of technical program management working directly with software engineering teams experience
- Experience managing programs across cross functional teams, building processes and coordinating release schedules
jobs.description:
The Amazon Robotics Global Edge Compute team owns initiatives at the forefront of Amazon's robotics and automation efforts. Our services manage millions of robotics devices across thousands of locations worldwide, from the latest NVIDIA GPU offerings supporting AI perception efforts, to bleeding edge manipulation robotics systems. Our technologies enable Amazon to revolutionize its logistics and fulfillment operations by pushing the boundaries of what's possible in automation.

As part of our dynamic and forward-thinking team, you'll have the opportunity to work on mission-critical projects that drive Amazon's robotics strategy. We foster a culture that encourages personal and professional growth, empowering our team members to continually expand their skills and knowledge. Work-life balance is a priority for us, and we strive to create an environment where our team can thrive both professionally and personally.


Key job responsibilities

• Drive the planning, execution, and delivery of Infrastructure Automation across multiple engineering teams.
• Manage project schedules, risks, dependencies, and resource allocations to ensure timely delivery of high-quality solutions.
• Facilitate effective communication and alignment across teams, stakeholders, and leadership.
• Implement agile methodologies, processes, and tools to streamline project execution and promote continuous improvement.
• Mentor and coach team members, fostering a culture of innovation and continuous learning.
• Drive continuous improvement to enhance efficient execution, adapting and creating processes to incorporate AI augmented development, program tracking and reporting.
• Assess technical problems faced by customers to influence feature prioritization and development, ensuring they remain unblocked in their ability to onboard and fully utilize GEC products.

A day in the life
Your day begins by connecting with customers who are leveraging GEC products across Amazon's global fulfillment network. You'll provide technical guidance, diving deep into their goals and issues to chart the best path forward for resolution. Whether it's a team preparing to deploy a new robotics system or troubleshooting an edge case in their testing environment, you're their trusted advisor.
Throughout the day, you'll work closely with builder teams, providing hands-on guidance to ensure everyone stays aligned and executes efficiently. You're constantly monitoring progress toward feature deliveries and program milestones, using the tools and processes you've designed and implemented to maintain visibility across all workstreams. When unknowns surface or priorities shift—as they inevitably do in a fast-paced environment—you're quick to course-correct, keeping both the team and leadership informed and seeking interventions when necessary.
A significant portion of your time is dedicated to deep research into your customer base. You'll evaluate their needs and goals, ensuring that GEC product plans align with what customers actually need, delivered on their timelines. This customer-obsessed approach drives your roadmap selections and feature prioritization.
You're always thinking several steps ahead, proactively hunting for risks and gaps in planning before they can impact program objectives. By identifying and addressing potential issues early, you keep programs on track and maintain the high bar of execution that Amazon Robotics demands.

jobs.description:
AWS Infrastructure Services owns the design, planning, delivery, and operation of all AWS global infrastructure. In other words, we’re the people who keep the cloud running. We support all AWS data centers and all of the servers, storage, networking, power, and cooling equipment that ensure our customers have continual access to the innovation they rely on. We work on the most challenging problems, with thousands of variables impacting the supply chain — and we’re looking for talented people who want to help.

You’ll join a diverse team of software, hardware, and network engineers, supply chain specialists, security experts, operations managers, and other vital roles. You’ll collaborate with people across AWS to help us deliver the highest standards for safety and security while providing seemingly infinite capacity at the lowest possible cost for our customers. And you’ll experience an inclusive culture that welcomes bold ideas and empowers you to own them to completion.

We are seeking a Business Intelligence Engineer professional in Seattle, WA who can operate with a startup mentality while partnering with managers and business leaders to deliver sound, data-backed recommendations and insights, identify areas of improvement and suggest and implement solutions.

The ideal candidate will be highly analytical, excited about data mining and generate insights that will influence the direction the organization. You will be curious and comfortable digging deep into data and developing core metrics to identify areas of improvement and partner with the business to find effective solutions. You will develop new ways to measure and track our performance, and improve the customer experience by building robust technical and analytical solutions to help us better understand customer needs. You will empower data driven decision making through implementing principles of data mining, data modelling, and analytical skills to define and measure metrics for evaluating health of the business. You will be able to extract, integrate and work on critical data to build out data pipelines, automate reports and dashboards to communicate your findings and leverage self-service tools to internal stakeholders.

Key job responsibilities
• Design, develop, implement, test, document, and operate large-scale, high-volume, high-performance data structures for business intelligence analytics.
• Implementing data structures using best practices in data modeling to provide on-line reporting and analysis using business intelligence tools and a logical abstraction layer against large, multi-dimensional datasets and multiple sources.
• Gathering business and functional requirements and translating the gathered requirements into robust, scalable, operable solutions that work well within the overall data architecture.
• Analyzing source data systems and driving best practices in source teams.
• Analyzing large amounts of data, discovering and solving real world problems, and building metrics and business cases to help business teams to make decisions.
• Participating in the full development life cycle, end-to-end, from design, implementation and testing, to documentation, delivery, support, and maintenance to produce comprehensive, usable dataset documentation and metadata.
• Evaluating and making decisions around dataset implementations designed and proposed by peer data engineers. Identifying business process improvements from the data level.
• Analyzing and evaluating business process, and through data driven insights proposing and implementing business process improvement.

About the team
Diverse Experiences
AWS values diverse experiences. Even if you do not meet all of the preferred qualifications and skills listed in the job description, we encourage candidates to apply. If your career is just starting, hasn’t followed a traditional path, or includes alternative experiences, don’t let it stop you from applying.

Why AWS?
Amazon Web Services (AWS) is the world’s most comprehensive and broadly adopted cloud platform. We pioneered cloud computing and never stopped innovating — that’s why customers from the most successful startups to Global 500 companies trust our robust suite of products and services to power their businesses.

Inclusive Team Culture
AWS values curiosity and connection. Our employee-led and company-sponsored affinity groups promote inclusion and empower our people to take pride in what makes us unique. Our inclusion events foster stronger, more collaborative teams. Our continual innovation is fueled by the bold ideas, fresh perspectives, and passionate voices our teams bring to everything we do.

Mentorship & Career Growth
We’re continuously raising our performance bar as we strive to become Earth’s Best Employer. That’s why you’ll find endless knowledge-sharing, mentorship and other career-advancing resources here to help you develop into a better-rounded professional.

Work/Life Balance
We value work-life harmony. Achieving success at work should never come at the expense of sacrifices at home, which is why we strive for flexibility as part of our working culture. When we feel supported in the workplace and at home, there’s nothing we can’t achieve.

jobs.basic_qualifications:
- 4+ years of working in mechanical engineering or equivalent experience
- Experience with DFx (Design for cost, test, manufacturing)
- Bachelor's degree in robotics, mechanical/mechatronics engineering, systems engineering or related field
- Experience that includes strong analytical skills, attention to detail, and effective communication abilities
- Experience working in a fast-paced and highly cross-functional organization
- Experience communicating technical concepts and processes using clear, simple language and visuals
- Experience in a highly agile environment
- SolidWorks or similar CAD experience, understanding of best practices for part design, advanced assemblies and complex configuration management
- Hands on experience crafting proof-of-concept models and prototypes
- Thorough understanding of technical principals, theories and concepts in hardware development and ability to apply them to product design
- Experience with full product lifecycle and proficiency at completing tasks to meet milestones
- Ability to support domestic and international travel as required by the program.
jobs.description:
Working at Amazon Robotics Are you inspired by invention? Is problem solving through teamwork in your DNA? Do you like the idea of seeing how your work impacts the bigger picture? Answer yes to any of these and you’ll fit right in here at Amazon Robotics. We are a smart team of doers that work passionately to apply advances in robotics and software to solve real-world problems that will transform our customers’ experiences in ways we can’t even image yet. We invent new improvements every day. We are Amazon Robotics and we will give you the tools and support you need to invent with us in ways that are rewarding, fulfilling and fun.

As an Amazon Robotics mechanical engineer, you will develop innovative hardware designs for our emerging robotic systems and improve existing hardware to optimize performance and enhance customer experience. The ideal candidate has experience designing/analyzing robots, enjoys analytical problem solving, possesses practical knowledge of high volume manufacturing methods and is a role model for engineering best practices. This position reports directly to the Hardware Development Engineering Manager.

Key job responsibilities
The Mechanical Engineer will be responsible for:
- Working with an interdisciplinary team to execute product designs from concept to production including specification, design, prototyping, validation and testing
- Working with the Operations, Manufacturing, Supply Chain and Quality organizations as well as vendors to ensure a smooth product launch and sustaining
- Writing functional specifications, design verification plans, and functional test procedures
- Exhibit role model behaviors of thorough engineering analysis and cradle to grave ownership
- Design and adaptation of holistic hardware solutions for ambiguous problems with fluid requirements

A day in the life
Amazon offers a full range of benefits that support you and eligible family members, including domestic partners. Benefits can vary by location, the number of regularly scheduled hours you work, length of employment, and job status such as seasonal or temporary employment. The benefits that generally apply to regular, full-time employees include:
1. Medical, Dental, and Vision Coverage
2. Maternity and Parental Leave Options
3. Paid Time Off (PTO)
4. 401(k) Plan

If you are not sure that every qualification on the list above describes you exactly, we'd still love to hear from you! At Amazon, we value people with unique backgrounds, experiences, and skillsets. If you’re passionate about this role and want to make an impact on a global scale, please apply!

jobs.title: Mechanical Engineer

jobs.basic_qualifications:
- Bachelor's degree
- Experience owning/driving roadmap strategy and definition
- Experience with feature delivery and tradeoffs of a product
- Experience technical product management
jobs.description:
Join us at the forefront of Amazon's sustainability initiatives to work on environmental and social advancements to support Amazon's long term worldwide sustainability strategy. At Amazon, we're working to be the most customer-centric company on earth.

To get there, we need exceptionally talented, bright, and driven people. The Worldwide Sustainability (WWS) organization capitalizes on Amazon’s scale and speed to build a more resilient and sustainable company. We manage our social and environmental impacts globally, and drive solutions that enable our customers, businesses, and the world to become more sustainable. The Worldwide Environment team utilizes science-based approaches to lower Amazon’s environmental impacts across the operations of our business units and products, making meaningful and measurable impacts in the communities in which we operate.

We are seeking a Principal Product Manager-Technical, Sustainable Water Specialist to serve as a deep subject matter expert (SME) in emerging technologies to conserve, recycle, harvest, and reuse water at scale, especially within industrial settings and within the operations that support Amazon’s value chain from our direct operations back to the commodity sourcing level. The ideal candidate will engage with third-party companies and startups to select, evaluate, and pilot water sustainability technologies and tools.

As the technical expert, you will drive these pilots toward large-scale deployment to support Amazon's water positive goals while partnering with external companies and in-house engineering teams to develop products that address Amazon's water technology needs. You will advise Amazon's businesses on developing their own water technology pilots and distribute centralized guidance on evolving capabilities in sustainable water use.

Working closely with Science and Tech teams, you will develop and deploy metrics and measurement methodologies to track the performance of existing and new water technologies within operations.

To succeed in this role, you will create and align leadership and businesses on long-term strategic water technology plans that address the changing water scarcity and risk landscape. You will serve as a visible leader in development and deployment within Worldwide Sustainability.

Key job responsibilities
- Serve as a company expert on hardware and software (including AI) technologies that enable sustainable water use at scale.
- Survey the emerging water technology landscape (including. e.g., startups serving various sectors, new tech offered by established water utilities, and technology being used in large-scale corporate replenishment projects) for technology assessment/piloting opportunities to benefit the business.
- Develop and align with leadership on comprehensive 3-5 year strategic plans to evolve Amazon’s water technology landscape across all businesses.
- Collaborate with Science and Tech teams to translate business needs (including growth plans in water scarce regions or regions with water regulation) into business- and region-customized water technology roadmaps and company-wide targets.
- Collaborate with Tech to develop tools for enterprise-wide integration of new tools.
- Use data from pilot programs to develop long-term company-wide targets that scale successful initiatives.
- Advise businesses on customized water technology needs and solutions, looking around corners for expanding regional and local regulations and water security issues.
- Represent the organization in industry forums and sustainability initiatives related to water.

About the team
Diverse Experiences: World Wide Sustainability values diverse experiences. Even if you do not meet all of the qualifications and skills listed in the job description, we encourage candidates to apply. If your career is just starting, hasn’t followed a traditional path, or includes alternative experiences, don’t let it stop you from applying.

Inclusive Team Culture:
It’s in our nature to learn and be curious. Our employee-led affinity groups foster a culture of inclusion that empower us to be proud of our differences. Ongoing events and learning experiences, including our Conversations on Race and Ethnicity (CORE) and AmazeCon (inclusive diversity) conferences, inspire us to never stop embracing our uniqueness.

Mentorship & Career Growth:
We’re continuously raising our performance bar as we strive to become Earth’s Best Employer. That’s why you’ll find endless knowledge-sharing, mentorship and other career-advancing resources here to help you develop into a better-rounded professional.

jobs.basic_qualifications:
- 5+ years of technical product or program management experience

jobs.description:
Join AWS as a Technical Program Manager and scale the design system that powers customer experiences across AWS digital properties. In this role, you'll connect product, engineering, design, and partner teams across the organization, driving alignment to deliver experiences that millions of AWS customers use daily.

Design systems sit at the intersection of design, engineering, and product, which means navigating multiple stakeholders, competing priorities, and complex dependencies. You'll bring it all together by working closely with engineers to drive features to completion, building the processes and mechanisms that enable the team to scale, and communicating the impact of what we're building to the broader organization.

We're seeking someone who influences without authority and navigates complex organizational dynamics. You'll align stakeholders across multiple teams, manage dependencies that span organizations, and build consensus among groups with different priorities.

Key job responsibilities
Partner with engineering to drive features to completion, removing blockers and ensuring timely delivery
Dive deep into technical implementations to understand constraints and drive clarity with engineering and product teams
Establish scalable mechanisms and processes that enable the team to operate efficiently as it grows
Own communications about the team's work, from release notes to leadership updates to organizational announcements
Drive cross-functional alignment and facilitate decision-making among stakeholders with competing priorities
Serve as the primary coordinator between design system consumers and contributors, building relationships that drive adoption
Lead cross-organizational initiatives, managing dependencies and driving consensus across team boundaries
Identify and mitigate risks through proactive stakeholder engagement and transparent communication

About the team
We build and maintain the design systems and platforms that power AWS Marketing's digital presence, serving millions of customers worldwide. We also support key events and experiences across the organization. The team is composed of front-end engineers, QA engineers, and design technologists, and we work closely with product and design partners in the same organization. We're constantly learning and experimenting with new technologies to bring the latest and greatest to customers. We value speed, autonomy, delivery, and delightful customer experiences.

jobs.basic_qualifications:

jobs.description:
Amazon's North America Reliability Maintenance & Engineering (RME) team needs a dynamic Regional Maintenance Manager to lead our operations. This key position will drive safety initiatives and guide field teams in developing effective decision-making tools for site managers. You'll evaluate Fulfillment Center RME departments' performance and implement proven practices to improve reliability metrics. Your mission: champion workplace safety, maximize equipment uptime, and optimize operational costs in our fast-paced fulfillment network.
The ideal candidate brings extensive expertise in facilities maintenance, workplace safety, and data-driven leadership. Experience with Material Handling Equipment (MHE) systems, packaging automation, distribution processes, and storage systems is valuable. We need a self-starter who excels at managing multiple complex projects while building strong relationships across all organizational levels. The role requires oversight of maintenance acceptance for large-scale projects and the ability to provide technical leadership to facilities maintenance teams.

Key Responsibilities
• Champion safety standards and drive team compliance
• Lead and mentor field-based Facilities Managers to expand and improve Amazon's North American Fulfillment Network
• Drive maintenance excellence through innovation, documentation, standardization, and safety practices
• Guide senior leadership on maintenance and equipment end of life (EOL) decisions using ROI analysis
• Oversee budget development, execution, and capital planning
• Manage vendor relationships and maximize network economies of scale
• Monitor and audit maintenance execution and safety practices across sites
• Coordinate maintenance teams across North America
• Develop and manage contract documents, including RFPs and purchase orders
• Support Fulfillment Center General Managers in facilities management
• Recruit and hire exceptional talent for Facilities Maintenance roles
• Build a world-class facilities maintenance organization
• Relocate as needed for Amazon's business needs
